title: Nurse Anesthetists
description: Administer anesthesia, monitor patient's vital signs, and oversee patient recovery from anesthesia. May assist anesthesiologists, surgeons, other physicians, or dentists. Must be registered nurses who have specialized graduate education.

tasks.text: Manage patients' airway or pulmonary status, using techniques such as endotracheal intubation, mechanical ventilation, pharmacological support, respiratory therapy, and extubation.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Respond to emergency situations by providing airway management, administering emergency fluids or drugs, or using basic or advanced cardiac life support techniques.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor patients' responses, including skin color, pupil dilation, pulse, heart rate, blood pressure, respiration, ventilation, or urine output, using invasive and noninvasive techniques.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select, order, or administer anesthetics, adjuvant drugs, accessory drugs, fluids or blood products as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select, prepare, or use equipment, monitors, supplies, or drugs for the administration of anesthetics.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Assess patients' medical histories to predict anesthesia response.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform or manage regional anesthetic techniques, such as local, spinal, epidural, caudal, nerve blocks and intravenous blocks.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop anesthesia care plans.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Obtain informed consent from patients for anesthesia procedures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare prescribed solutions and administer local, intravenous, spinal, or other anesthetics, following specified methods and procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform pre-anesthetic screenings, including physical evaluations and patient interviews, and document results.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Calibrate and test anesthesia equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Evaluate patients' post-surgical or post-anesthesia responses, taking appropriate corrective actions or requesting consultation if complications occur.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Administer post-anesthesia medications or fluids to support patients' cardiovascular systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select and prescribe post-anesthesia medications or treatments to patients.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform or evaluate the results of diagnostic tests, such as radiographs (x-rays) and electrocardiograms (EKGs).
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select, order, or administer pre-anesthetic medications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Insert peripheral or central intravenous catheters.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Insert arterial catheters or perform arterial punctures to obtain arterial blood samples.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Discharge patients from post-anesthesia care.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Read current literature, talk with colleagues, and participate in professional organizations or conferences to keep abreast of developments in nursing.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Request anesthesia equipment repairs, adjustments, or safety tests.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Instruct nurses, residents, interns, students, or other staff on topics such as anesthetic techniques, pain management and emergency responses.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Disassemble and clean anesthesia equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
